system.description:
The party has chosen to flee rather than fight the Pale Maw at Beinvaka. This is the Mad Dash — a hard, fast escape through a hostile enclave. The Pale Maw know they're here (or almost do). The party must move through Beinvaka's streets, through their checkpoint, and north without being stopped. Total success means they escape undetected and Þráinn cannot alert forces north. Failure means combat, and the alert goes north immediately.

Director interlude before rolling: "The guard looks directly at you... then their gaze slides away toward the southern approach." This is the window the party uses.

complications.round1:
Moving through Beinvaka undetected while Pale Maw soldiers are alert on the perimeter. The town is small but the cultists know every face — strangers are visible. The party must move in the window created by the guard's distracted gaze, before the routine check resumes.

  • Agility: Move through the back streets of Beinvaka at speed, avoiding the main plaza where Þráinn has positioned himself.
  • Intuition: Read patrol patterns and identify which guards are ritual-distracted (some cultists perform evening rites facing the inverted fire-pit).
  • Reason: Identify an optimal exit route from the town based on the entry approach — which roads lead north without passing the main gate?
  • Presence: If the party must pass near a guard, appear calm and purposeful — not fleeing. Presence vs. Intuition opposed test.
complications.round2:
The northern checkpoint: a gate with two Veterans and three Soldiers. Beyond it: open road. The party must either bluff through, bypass entirely, or overwhelm the guards silently before the alert sounds. Þráinn's position is 200ft behind them — close enough to hear a shout.

  • Agility: Bypass the checkpoint entirely by going over or around the gate structure (15ft fence, guard on top).
  • Might: Force the gate silently — remove the bar without it falling and making noise (requires teamwork: 2 heroes working the bar simultaneously).
  • Presence: Bluff the gate guards — produce some element of legitimacy ("We were sent north by the Inquisitor"). Presence vs. Intuition (opposed).
  • Reason: Recall that Pale Maw checkpoints use a specific sigil-token system — if anyone has seen or can replicate one, the guard defers automatically.

outcomes.round1:
Total Success R1: The party is through the residential quarter without being spotted. One guard sees movement but attributes it to wind.
Partial Success R1: The party moves but one hero is briefly spotted. They must make a fast choice: hide (Agility test, if failed: 1 Pale Maw Soldier investigates) or keep moving (Presence, if failed: alarm raised 30 seconds early).
Failure R1: A cultist specifically spots Kaelen's ember-mark glowing faintly in the dark. Alarm raised immediately — combat begins at the checkpoint gate.
outcomes.round2:
Total Success: The party clears the checkpoint clean. They are past Beinvaka. Behind them, moments later, Þráinn's psychic wail: "The heretic flees! The Keeper runs north! Mobilize!" But the party is already past the gate, running, and the wail goes in the wrong direction. Grafvitnir's network receives incomplete information. The party arrives at Surt's Forge without a prepared enemy.
Partial Success: The party gets through but the gate alarm sounds just as the last hero clears it. Þráinn's alert goes north. The party is clear but the Pale Maw network is fully mobilized — Beat 18 Strike Team arrives with more information and a shorter response window.
Failure: Caught at the gate. Full combat: 2 Veterans + 3 Soldiers at gate, Þráinn and 4 Soldiers approaching from 200ft. Party must fight through or surrender (surrender = campaign failure state).
